   10 w1120-page   Pic 9(03).
  05 w1200-heading2.
   10 filler   Pic x(33) value spaces.
   10 filler   Pic x(14)
       value "Match Gradings".
   10 filler   Pic x(33) value spaces.
  05 w1400-control1.
   10 w1410-match-name  Pic x(29).
   10 filler   Pic x(51) value spaces.
  05 w1500-detailheading.
   10 w1510-detailheading1.
    15 filler   Pic x(22) value spaces.
    15 filler   Pic x(04) value "Name".
    15 filler   Pic x(16) value spaces.
    15 filler   Pic x(10)
       value "last three".
    15 filler   Pic x(11) value spaces.
    15 filler   Pic x(08)
       value "Personal".
    15 filler   Pic x(20) value spaces.
   10 w1520-detailheading2.
    15 filler   Pic x(13) value spaces.
    15 filler   Pic x(07)
       value "Surname".
    15 filler   Pic x(09) value spaces.
    15 filler   Pic x(05) value "First".
    15 filler   Pic x(09) value spaces.
    15 filler   Pic x(06) value "Scores".
    15 filler   Pic x(05) value spaces.
    15 filler   Pic x(08)
       value "handicap".
    15 filler   Pic x(02) value spaces.
    15 filler   Pic x(04) value "Best".
    15 filler   Pic x(02) value spaces.
    15 filler   Pic x(05) value "Grade".
    15 filler   Pic x(05) value spaces.
  05 w1600-detail-line.
   10 filler   Pic x(09) value spaces.
   10 w1620-lname   Pic x(15).
   10 filler   Pic x value spaces.
   10 w1610-fname   Pic x(15).
   10 filler   Pic x(02) value spaces.
   10 w1630-score1   Pic 9(03).
   10 filler   Pic x value spaces.
   10 w1640-score2   Pic 9(03).
   10 filler   Pic x value spaces.
   10 w1650-score3   Pic 9(03).
   10 filler   Pic x(05) value spaces.
   10 w1660-hcap   Pic 9(03).
   10 filler   Pic x(05) value spaces.
   10 w1670-pbest   Pic 9(03).
   10 filler   Pic x(05) value spaces.
   10 w1680-grade   Pic x.
   10 filler   Pic x(06) value spaces.
 SCREEN SECTION.
*---------------
 01 ss100-Main-screen.
  05 BLANK SCREEN.
  05 LINE 1 COLUMN 01 value "Kangaroo Island Pistol Club".
  05  COLUMN 64 value "DATE: ".
  05  COLUMN 70 Pic x(10) from w220-Date.
 01 ss200-first-entries.
  05 ss210-enter-match.
   10 LINE 3 COLUMN 10 value "Match: ".
   10  COLUMN 17 Pic x(29) FROM
      w711-match(match-selection).
   10 LINE 24 COLUMN 65 Pic x TO selector auto.
 01 ss500-details-needed.
  05 ss510-header.
   10 LINE 8 COLUMN 19 value "Name".
   10  COLUMN 54 value "Stage".
   10 LINE 9 COLUMN 10 value "Frist".
   10  COLUMN 27 value "Last".
   10  COLUMN 40 value "Calibra".
   10  COLUMN 49 value "  ".
   10  COLUMN 68 value "Score".
  05 ss520-details.
   10 LINE 10 COLUMN 5 Pic x(15) USING w511-mname-first.
   10  COLUMN 25 Pic x(15) USING w512-mname-last.
  05 ss530-calibra.
   10  COLUMN 42 Pic x(03) FROM
    w713b-cal-type(match-selection, cal-selection).
   10 LINE 24 COLUMN 65 Pic x TO selector auto.
  05 ss540-aggregate.
   10 LINE 10 COLUMN 69 Pic 9(03) USING f390-aggregate.
 01 ss900-msg-screen.
  05 LINE 22 COLUMN 01 value "MESSAGE: ".
  05 LINE 23 COLUMN 10 Pic x(70) FROM w990-display-message.
  05 LINE 24 COLUMN 01 Pic x(80) FROM w980-display-action.
  05 LINE 24 COLUMN 65 Pic x TO w140-press-a-key.
 PROCEDURE DIVISION.
********************
 0000000-MAIN-MODULE.
*====================
  PERFORM 1000000-INITIALISE-MODULE.
  PERFORM 2000000-PROCESS-MODULE UNTIL w111-eof.
  PERFORM 3000000-END-MODULE.
  STOP RUN.
 1000000-INITIALISE-MODULE.
*==========================
  PERFORM 1300000-SET-DATE.
  PERFORM 1400000-INITIALISE-VARS.
  PERFORM 1500000-READ-INITIAL-FILE.
 1300000-SET-DATE.
*=================
* It chocks on this...
  MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO w210-system-date.
  STRING w213-day "/"
   w212-month "/"
   w211-year
   DELIMITED BY SIZE
    INTO w220-Date
  END-STRING.
 1400000-INITIALISE-VARS.
*========================
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
  INITIALIZE w500-members-rec
   f300-scores-rec.
  OPEN INPUT f700-initial-file
   INPUT f500-calibras-file
   INPUT f100-matches-file
   I-O f300-scores-file
   I-O f400-pbhcap-file
   OUTPUT f900-report-file.
  PERFORM 141000-LOAD-MATCH-ARRAY
   VARYING w710-match-inx FROM 1 BY 1
    UNTIL w710-match-inx>15 OR w111-eof.
  MOVE 1 TO match-selection.
  PERFORM 141110-LOAD-CALIBRA-ARRAY
   VARYING w713-cal-inx FROM 1 BY 1
    UNTIL w713-cal-inx>11 OR w111-eof.
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
 141000-LOAD-MATCH-ARRAY.
*========================
  READ f100-matches-file
   AT END
    MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
   NOT AT END
    PERFORM 141100-LOAD-MATCH
  END-READ.
 141100-LOAD-MATCH.
*==================
  MOVE f110-matches-match TO w711-match(w710-match-inx).
  MOVE f180-matches-max TO w711d-out-of(w710-match-inx).
  MOVE f120-grades-gradem TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 1).
  MOVE f130-grades-gradea TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 2).
  MOVE f140-grades-gradeb TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 3).
  MOVE f150-grades-gradec TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 4).
  MOVE f160-match-cal177 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 1).
  MOVE f160-match-cal22s TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 2).
  MOVE f160-match-cal22l TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 3).
  MOVE f160-match-calcf TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 4).
  MOVE f160-match-cal32 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 5).
  MOVE f160-match-cal38 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 6).
  MOVE f160-match-cal357 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 7).
  MOVE f160-match-cal9mm TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 8).
  MOVE f160-match-cal44 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 9).
  MOVE f160-match-cal45 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 10).
  MOVE f170-match-stages TO w711a-stages(w710-match-inx).
  MOVE f171-mstage1-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx, 1).
  MOVE f172-mstage2-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx, 2).
  MOVE f173-mstage3-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx, 3).
  MOVE f174-mstage4-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx, 4).
  MOVE "PRA" TO w713b-cal-type(w710-match-inx, 11).
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
 141110-LOAD-CALIBRA-ARRAY.
*==========================
  READ f500-calibras-file
   AT END
    MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
   NOT AT END
    PERFORM 141111-LOAD-CALIBRA
     VARYING w710-match-inx FROM 1 BY 1
      UNTIL w710-match-inx>15
    MOVE "PRA" TO
     w713b-cal-type(w710-match-inx, w713-cal-inx)
  END-READ.
 141111-LOAD-CALIBRA.
*====================
  MOVE f510-calibra TO
    w713b-cal-type(w710-match-inx, w713-cal-inx).
 1500000-READ-INITIAL-FILE.
*==========================
  READ f700-initial-file
   AT END
    MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
  END-READ.
 2000000-PROCESS-MODULE.
*=======================
  MOVE f741-day TO f313c-day.
  MOVE f742-month TO f313b-month.
  MOVE f743-year TO f313a-year.
  MOVE f710-first TO f311a-sname-first
    f408a-fname.
  MOVE f710-last TO f311b-sname-last
    f408a-lname.
  MOVE f720-imatch TO f312-match-shot
    match-selection
    f409-match.
  MOVE f730-cal TO f340-calibra.
  MOVE f750-stage1 TO f350-stage1.
  MOVE f760-stage2 TO f360-stage2.
  MOVE f770-stage3 TO f370-stage3.
  MOVE f780-stage4 TO f380-stage4.
  MOVE f790-score TO f390-aggregate.
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
  PERFORM 2530000-READ-PBHCAP-FILE
  MOVE f460-hcap TO f395-hcap-attime.
  PERFORM 2540000-WRITE-SCORE.
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
  MOVE ZEROS TO f390-aggregate.
  PERFORM 1500000-READ-INITIAL-FILE.
 2530000-READ-PBHCAP-FILE.
*=========================
  READ f400-pbhcap-file
   INVALID KEY
    IF(w120-file-status EQUALS 23)
    THEN
     MOVE w711d-out-of(match-selection) TO f460-hcap
     MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
     PERFORM 2533000-VALID-CALIBRA
      VARYING cal-selection FROM 1 BY 1
       UNTIL cal-selection>10 OR w111-eof
     IF(w111-eof)
     THEN
      MOVE ZEROS TO f450-pbest
       f440-score3
       f430-score2
       f420-score1
      MOVE "D" TO f470-grade
      WRITE f400-pbhcap-rec
       INVALID KEY
        MOVE "error writing pbhcap "
         TO w980-display-action
        PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
        PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
      END-WRITE
     ELSE
      MOVE "PRA" TO f340-calibra
     END-IF
     MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
    ELSE
     MOVE "error reading pbhcap "
       TO w980-display-action
   PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
   END-IF
  END-READ.
 2533000-VALID-CALIBRA.
*======================
  IF((w713a-cal(match-selection, cal-selection)>ZEROS) AND
  (w713b-cal-type(match-selection, cal-selection) EQUALS
    f340-calibra))
  THEN
  MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
  END-IF.
 2540000-WRITE-SCORE.
*====================
  WRITE f300-scores-rec
  INVALID KEY
   MOVE "error writing score " TO w980-display-action
   PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
  NOT INVALID KEY
   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
   PERFORM 2531000-PBHCAP
  END-WRITE.
 2531000-PBHCAP.
*===============
  PERFORM 2530000-READ-PBHCAP-FILE.
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
  PERFORM 2533000-VALID-CALIBRA
  VARYING cal-selection FROM 1 BY 1
   UNTIL cal-selection>10 OR w111-eof.
  IF(NOT w111-eof)
  THEN
  MOVE "PRA" TO f340-calibra
  END-IF.
  IF(f340-calibra NOT EQUAL "PRA")
  THEN
  IF(f390-aggregate>f450-pbest)
  THEN
   MOVE f390-aggregate TO f450-pbest
   PERFORM 2531200-SET-GRADE
  END-IF
  PERFORM 2531300-CAL-HCAP
  PERFORM 2531400-REWRITE-PBHCAP-FILE
  END-IF.
 2531200-SET-GRADE.
*==================
  IF(f390-aggregate<w712b-grade(match-selection, 1))
  THEN
  MOVE "A" TO f470-grade
  IF(f390-aggregate<w712b-grade(match-selection, 2))
  THEN
   MOVE "B" TO f470-grade
   IF(f390-aggregate<w712b-grade(match-selection, 3))
   THEN
   MOVE "C" TO f470-grade
   IF
    (f390-aggregate<w712b-grade(match-selection,4))
   THEN
    MOVE "D" TO f470-grade
   END-IF
   END-IF
  END-IF
  ELSE
  MOVE "M" TO f470-grade
  END-IF.
 2531300-CAL-HCAP.
*=================
  MOVE f430-score2 TO f420-score1.
  MOVE f440-score3 TO f430-score2.
  IF(f430-score2>ZERO AND f420-score1>ZERO)
  THEN
  ADD f390-aggregate
   f420-score1
   f430-score2 GIVING w320-temp-hcap
  DIVIDE w320-temp-hcap BY 3 GIVING w320-temp-hcap
  SUBTRACT w320-temp-hcap
   FROM w711d-out-of(match-selection)
  GIVING w320-temp-hcap
  ELSE
  MOVE w711d-out-of(match-selection) TO w320-temp-hcap
  END-IF.
  MOVE f390-aggregate TO f440-score3.
  MOVE f460-hcap TO f395-hcap-attime
  IF(w320-temp-hcap<f460-hcap)
  THEN
  MOVE w320-temp-hcap TO f460-hcap
  END-IF.
 2531400-REWRITE-PBHCAP-FILE.
*============================
  REWRITE f400-pbhcap-rec
  INVALID KEY
   MOVE "error rewriting pbhcap "
       TO w980-display-action
   PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
  END-REWRITE.
 2900000-ERROR.
*==============
  MOVE FUNCTION LENGTH(a-to-continue) TO w311-lenA.
  MOVE FUNCTION LENGTH(w980-display-action) TO w312-lenB.
  COMPUTE w313-center=(w312-lenB - w311-lenA)/2.
  MOVE a-to-continue TO w980-display-action(w313-center:).
  PERFORM 2999999-DISPLAY-MESG.
 2888888-FILE-STATUS.
*====================
  EVALUATE w120-file-status
  WHEN 22
   STRING w980-display-action
   DELIMITED BY " "
    " duplicate key"
   DELIMITED BY SIZE
    INTO w990-display-message
   END-STRING
  WHEN OTHER
   STRING w980-display-action
   DELIMITED BY " "
    " "
   DELIMITED BY SIZE
    w120-file-status
   DELIMITED BY SIZE
    INTO w990-display-message
   END-STRING
  END-EVALUATE.
  MOVE SPACES TO w980-display-action.
 2999999-DISPLAY-MESG.
*=====================
  DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
  ACCEPT ss900-msg-screen.
  MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
    w980-display-action.
 3000000-END-MODULE.
*===================
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
  MOVE 1 TO match-selection.
  PERFORM UNTIL w111-eof
  DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen
  MOVE "n" TO w160-cancel
  PERFORM 3100000-ENTER-MATCH UNTIL w111-eof
  IF(NOT w161-cancel)
  THEN
   MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
   PERFORM 3600000-GRADINGS
  END-IF
  MOVE a-anymore TO w980-display-action
  MOVE SPACES TO w990-Display-message
  PERFORM 2999999-DISPLAY-MESG
  MOVE w140-press-a-key TO w110-end-of-file
  IF(NOT w111-eof)
  THEN
   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
  ELSE
   MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
  END-IF
  END-PERFORM.
  CLOSE f300-scores-file
   f400-pbhcap-file
   f700-initial-file
   f900-report-file.
 3100000-ENTER-MATCH.
*====================
  DISPLAY ss210-enter-match.
  MOVE i1 TO w990-display-message.
  MOVE i2 TO w980-display-action.
  DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
  MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
    w980-display-action.
  ACCEPT ss210-enter-match.
  EVALUATE TRUE
  WHEN sup
   PERFORM 2110000-MOVE-MATCH-UP
  WHEN sdown
   PERFORM 2120000-MOVE-MATCH-DOWN
  WHEN scancel
   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
   MOVE "y" TO w160-cancel
  WHEN sselect
   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
   MOVE "n" TO w160-cancel
  END-EVALUATE.
  MOVE SPACES TO selector.
 2110000-MOVE-MATCH-UP.
*======================
  IF(match-selection>=15)
  THEN
  MOVE 1 TO match-selection
  ELSE
  ADD 1 TO match-selection
  END-IF.
 2120000-MOVE-MATCH-DOWN.
*========================
  IF(match-selection<=1)
  THEN
  MOVE 15 TO match-selection
  ELSE
  SUBTRACT 1 FROM match-selection
  END-IF.
 3600000-GRADINGS.
*=================
  PERFORM 2610000-INITIAL-REPORT
  IF(NOT w111-eof)
  THEN
  PERFORM 2620000-REPORT-HEADINGS
  PERFORM 2630000-REPORT-CONTROL-BREAK
  MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
  PERFORM 2640000-REPORT-DETAILS UNTIL w111-eof
  END-IF.
 2610000-INITIAL-REPORT.
*=======================
  MOVE ZEROS TO w1120-page.
  MOVE match-selection TO f409-match.
  PERFORM 2612000-MEMBER-KEY-INITIAL-SET.
 2612000-MEMBER-KEY-INITIAL-SET.
*===============================
  MOVE SPACES TO f408-mname.
  START f400-pbhcap-file
  KEY >= f410-indexed-key
  INVALID KEY
   MOVE
   "err starting pbhcap for report"
       TO w980-display-action
   PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
   MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
  PERFORM 2612100-NEXT-PBHCAP.
 2612100-NEXT-PBHCAP.
*====================
  READ f400-pbhcap-file NEXT RECORD
  AT END
   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
  END-READ.
 2620000-REPORT-HEADINGS.
*========================
  ADD 1 TO w1120-page.
  MOVE w220-Date TO w1110-date.
  WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1100-heading1
  AFTER ADVANCING PAGE.
  WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1200-heading2
  AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES.
  MOVE 6 TO w350-line-count.
  MOVE f409-match TO w360-old-match.
  MOVE w711-match(w360-old-match) TO w1410-match-name.
 2630000-REPORT-CONTROL-BREAK.
*=============================
  WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1400-control1
  AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES.
  WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1510-detailheading1
  AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES.
  WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1520-detailheading2
  AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINES.
  MOVE SPACES TO w1410-match-name.
  ADD 5 TO w350-line-count.
 2640000-REPORT-DETAILS.
*=======================
  IF(f409-match NOT EQUAL TO w360-old-match)
  THEN
  MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
  ELSE
  PERFORM 2641000-SET-UP-REPORT
  PERFORM 2642000-PRINT-REPORT
  PERFORM 2612100-NEXT-PBHCAP
  END-IF.
 2641000-SET-UP-REPORT.
*======================
  MOVE f408a-fname TO w1610-fname.
  MOVE f408a-lname TO w1620-lname.
  MOVE f420-score1 TO w1630-score1.
  MOVE f430-score2 TO w1640-score2.
  MOVE f440-score3 TO w1650-score3.
  MOVE f450-pbest TO w1670-pbest.
  MOVE f460-hcap TO w1660-hcap.
  MOVE f470-grade TO w1680-grade.
 2642000-PRINT-REPORT.
*=====================
  IF(w350-line-count>70)
  THEN
  PERFORM 2620000-REPORT-HEADINGS
  PERFORM 2630000-REPORT-CONTROL-BREAK
  END-IF.
  WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1600-detail-line
  AFTER ADVANCING 1 LINES.
  ADD 1 TO w350-line-count.
**********************************************************
```

<a id="t-b51ea8d00d"></a>
## Thread: Fujitsu Tools — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Fujitsu Tools

- **From:** "Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-25T13:44:44+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<gfHZ6.304022$NK4.21657959@news2.aus1.giganews.com>`

```
OK, we've got a couple of tools:

1. A PowerCobol project to adjust MajorVersion, MinorVersion, and
ReleaseNumber to a bevy of PPJ files so they don't have to be opened
one-by-one simply to upgrade the version/release number. Maj.Min.Rel
numbers for 100 projects can be adjusted in ten seconds with no button
pushes or mouse-clicks. Credit to FJ technical support for providing
the clues for this technique.

2. Finally figured out the batch-compile gizmo so that one batch file
can compile 100 projects without intervention (this takes a bit longer
than ten seconds).

Used together, they provide a trouble-free and more efficient
mechanism to generate a new product release.

Email me if you want copies.

Jerry Peacock
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu Tools

- **From:** "Editor" <Editor@aboutlegacycoding.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-26T09:51:53-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9ha44o$jlt$1@allnight.news.cais.net>`
- **References:** `<gfHZ6.304022$NK4.21657959@news2.aus1.giganews.com>`

```
Jerry,
We maintain a "download library" for the community at
www.aboutlegacycoding.com.  If you would like, we can make your routines
available for download there.

The system is set up such that you can recieve an optional e-mail (with the
downloaders e-mail address and name) everytime someone downloads your
routines (or not if you don't want the extra e-mail).

We have quite a few interesting routines in the library, many of them from
regular posters in the comp.lang.cobol newsgroup, but I don't think we have
anything quite like these.

If you would like to add them to the library, send me the routines
(preferably in one zip file for each, with any doc in the zip as a text
file), your full name (if you want or I can just use Jerry P.) a one or two
paragraph description of each routine, the e-mail address you would like to
use to be notified (or not) and a web address of your web site if you would
like.
OR you can go to www.aboutlegacycoding.com, visit the library and click on
the "Submit a Download" button.
You have to register to get to the Download section but I only use the
information to send 1 e-mail every 2 months to notify of new issues - a full
privacy statement is on the site.  You will still have to e-mail me the
routines, but this form will collect the info I need to post the routines.
```

---

<a id="t-2fb34f54e0"></a>
## Thread: SPLIT-KEYS in Fujitsu-Cobol — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### SPLIT-KEYS in Fujitsu-Cobol

- **From:** "SPECTRA" <Spectra.Soft@t-online.de>
- **Date:** 2001-06-25T20:22:15+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9h7vbh$4v3$07$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
During the conversion of Cobol-Sources from MF to Fujitsu-Cobol we ran into
a problem concerning the formal description of RECORD KEY and several
ALTERNATE KEYs  when both are splitted, (not contiguous in the record.).

It would help a lot having a sample-program  showing the declaration (SELECT
an FD) and the use (PROCEDURE - Read/write)  whit split-keys.

Can you help?

Thanks in advance!
Hans
```

#### ↳ Re: SPLIT-KEYS in Fujitsu-Cobol

- **From:** Carlos lages <member@dbforums.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-26T00:04:23+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8kQZ6.41040$G45.2128554@news1.onlynews.com>`
- **References:** `<9h7vbh$4v3$07$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
Hans in Mf Cobol the Key is something like that.

      Select Filein assig to Disk Organization is Indexed record key is
      ws-chave alternarte key is ws-name with duplicates alternate key
      is ws-cod = ws-field1 ws-field2
-----------------
    Read filein key is ws-cod


in Power Cobol

elect Filein assig to Disk Organization is Indexed record key is
ws-chave alternarte key is ws-name with duplicates alternate key is
ws-field1 ws-field2

Read filein key is ws-field1 ws-field2


The Diferrence in this case is thar you Type More

that is It

Carlos Lages Rio de Janeiro
```

---

<a id="t-479679d983"></a>
## Thread: Cobol and AFP — 3 msgs

### Cobol and AFP

- **From:** Keith Risinger <syskmr@nmsu.edu>
- **Date:** 2001-06-25T14:18:30-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B379C96.27878900@nmsu.edu>`

```
Has anyone done much using Cobol to create AFP documents? I see where
there is something
from Ibm called "AFP Toolbox" and was wondering if anyone is using this.
I'm not the one writing the code,
but I'm looking for sources of information for one of our programmers.
Any info would be appreciated.

TIA,
  Keith Risinger
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol and AFP

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-25T16:04:32-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9h890o$c2v$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<3B379C96.27878900@nmsu.edu>`

```
It certainly doesn't help you with how or what to do, but one thing that
some current IBM COBOL programmers don't know about is the (relatively new),

   WRITE xxx AFTER ADVANCING AFP-5A

syntax.  You might want to look at:


http://publibz.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr_OS390/BOOKS/igylr205/6.2.40.5
.4?

and from there, some of the books that it refers to.
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol and AFP

- **From:** Joseph Kohler <joe_kohler@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-25T17:06:26-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B37A7D2.1F88FCFD@yahoo.com>`
- **References:** `<3B379C96.27878900@nmsu.edu>`

```
Keith Risinger wrote:
> 
> Has anyone done much using Cobol to create AFP documents? I see where
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>   Keith Risinger

AFP is a forms programming language used to make nice laser printer
forms from flat files.  You basically write out a record of all the data
to appear on the report and the AFP programmer builds a form and maps
the data to the form.  The language is arcane and tied closely to page
position.  We had a guy here who did this full-time and he always
started by laying out the form on a transparency and then worked with
the data using the transparency to make final adjustments to the
position where each field should fall.

Joe
```

Hi,

the workstations
have Windows 95 and 98, there have never been a problem.  But, today I
upgraded a windows 98
to Windows Me and it do not run the application.  When I try to run the
program on the windows ME
computer the program stop and give me a 98,33 IO Error.  The programs is
running on the others
computers without any problem. Can anyone help me with this problem?

Thank You!

Note : I tried to get help from Liant/RM but they don't support this program
(they recommend me to upgrade to recent version).


Victor

wrote:

> Hi,
> 
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…
> 


I believe you have been bitten by the "ME has eliminated 'legacy' DOS 
support" feature (?)

Microsoft removed support for older DOS programs from Windows ME. Your
only real choice is to remove Windows ME and re-install Windows 98 (or
you can install IBMs OS/2 which will also support the DOS programs).
```

#### ↳ Re: Rm/Cobol program and WindowsMe!

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>

"Victor M. Arocho" wrote:

> Victor

I have Version 2.0b running under Win 98. The constant nightmare I had when
loading to a different PC is remembering the sequence to get the old DOS SETVER,
so that I hit on a Version of DOS that the RM compiler was happy with.

As you say you are getting file status 98, 33 - presumably you are able to
compile cleanly - i.e. you have the right SETVER(sion) ? Way out on a limb - is
there a difference between Win x and Win ME when it comes to number of files
that can be active in any one program ? This was always a problem for me
indicating a 98 - index crashed - which it hadn't - just hiccupped on one or
more files too many.

Seriously, if you are continuing with RM, don't nickel and dime - take their
advice and get an up to date version.

Jimmy
```

- **From:** "J MacRae" <jmacrae1@mbox.rrc.mb.ca>

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<iu2_6.649$K7.19304@news2.mts.net>`

```
Hi, I am new to sp2 and am having some trouble.
What I need to do is display an Inquiry Screen to get criteria from the user
and after retrieving the data redisplay the screen.
The user enters criteria and presses FIND, control is returned  to the COBOL
program in order to retrieve the record and move the data into the panel
fields.  The problem is that once the data is retrieved I cannot redisplay
the updated window.

I tried this:

CALL "SP2" USING SP2-DISPLAY-WINDOW SP2-NULL-PARM.

but no luck.

Any suggestions?  Thanks.
Jason.
```

```
I think Bob already answered you, but what you do is:

Call "Sp2" using sp2-converse-panel xxxxx-converse-data

and you will do the I/O on the panel.  Display doesn't "Get" any data.

## Thread: CICS Transaction Gateway Version 4.0 - and COBOL and PL/I — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### CICS Transaction Gateway Version 4.0 - and COBOL and PL/I

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-26T19:55:21-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9hbatm$jem$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I don't really understand what it means (or if it will have any "real world"
impact on anyone - today or in the future), but in case you missed it at:

 http://www.ibmlink.ibm.com/usalets&parms=H_201-187

It states (in part),

"The following functions of CICS Transaction Gateway V3 are no longer
supported:

 - Operation on the OS/2� platform
 - COBOL, PL/I, and REXX applications
 - The CICSTELD function
 - Novell Netware for SAA� "
```

#### ↳ Re: CICS Transaction Gateway Version 4.0 - and COBOL and PL/I

- **From:** thaneh@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2001-06-26T22:23:06-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bfdfc3e8.0106262123.cc224d@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<9hbatm$jem$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
The CICS transaction gateway never really took off and is obsolete
with the latest z/OS and Transaction Server.  There's no reason for it
to exist at this point.

"William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com> wrote in message news:<9hbatm$jem$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net>...
> I don't really understand what it means (or if it will have any "real world"
> impact on anyone - today or in the future), but in case you missed it at:
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>  - Novell Netware for SAAï¿½ "
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: CICS Transaction Gateway Version 4.0 - and COBOL and PL/I

- **From:** David Radley <david_radley@uk.ibm.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-27T11:46:12+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B39B974.BDB04B51@uk.ibm.com>`
- **References:** `<9hbatm$jem$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net> <bfdfc3e8.0106262123.cc224d@posting.google.com>`

```
The CTG is actually very popular and is the prime CICS connector to Websphere. The CTG gives easy access to COBOL
(and other) CICS applications using the latest technologies . I am aware of many largecompanies who rely on the CTG
to do their e-business on os/390 and other platforms. Many of these companies are still be running their CICS COBOL
application on the backend.

Thane Hubbell wrote:

> The CICS transaction gateway never really took off and is obsolete
> with the latest z/OS and Transaction Server.  There's no reason for it
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
> >  - Novell Netware for SAAï¿½ "

<a id="t-1cf5825dbc"></a>
## Thread: COBO to Java translation — 5 msgs

### COBO to Java translation

- **From:** Howard Wong <hwong@ican.net>
- **Date:** 2001-06-26T23:11:20-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B394ED8.EEBA7F03@ican.net>`

```
Hi All,

I'm working on a project that is looking into extracting some
functionality of any existing COBOL application and making it into a
stand-alone application. It will be enhanced and used to serve the needs
of other similar applications that the organization has.

The application is now on an MVS/IMS/IBM VS COBOL II platform. We are
contemplating on how best we can deploy this new application, because it
is part of an initiative to migrate our mainframe UI to browser-based
UI.

One way is to build this new app on the same host platform, and then
construct the JSP/HTML GUI to talk to the new app via MQ.

The other way is to build the new app in Java and put it on the Web
server, and it will in turn interface with the host applications via MQ.
There are two approaches that we are considering: one is to redesign and
write the application in Java, and two is to translate the COBOL code to
Java.

I'm conducting research into all of the above approaches. What I need
help with is the translation of COBOL to Java. By this I mean something
like going through the COBOL code line by line and replace that with
equivalent Java statements or new classes/methods. This is of course a
very simplistic view. There are things that will likely to provide
serious challenges, such as REDEFINE, FILLER, GOTO (I mean the really
bad ones), CORRESPONDING phrases, etc.

I have searched in Google with the phrase "cobol to java translation"
and got a bunch of company web sites promoting their own wares, plus an
odd review or two. What I am looking for is some real-life experience on
this subject. Has anyone attempted translating COBOL to Java? Did you
use any automation tool? How difficult was that (automated or
otherwise)? What were the "gotchas"?

As for those tools out there that can automate the job, has anyone used
any of them? If so, how effective were they? Was the generated Java code
tight and tidy (i.e. easy to maintain)?

One request: please forward you postings to my email address
"hhjwong@netscape.net" as well. I appologise for the inconvenience but I
can no longer get to any of my usual news servers at work, which I have
found out is due to firewall rule changes. I have been told that there
are no approved news servers that I can use. I'm posting this from home,
but I would like to get any repsonse ASAP, and I can get to my netscape
account at work. Besides, I really rather not bring any work home. ;-)

Many thanks in advance.

Regards,
Howard Wong
hhjwong@netscape.net
```

#### ↳ Re: COBO to Java translation

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-27T03:41:31+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B39559B.28EA568@home.com>`
- **References:** `<3B394ED8.EEBA7F03@ican.net>`

```


Howard Wong wrote:

> Hi All,
>
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> Howard,

I'm in no position to answer your specific questions but would offer a word
of caution as regards OO. (And I'm assuming you are already comfortable with
Java ?).

It is possible for me to do a 'quick and dirty' job in COBOL 'translating'
from non-OO to OO COBOL, but it is quick and dirty and means substituting
method-names for paragraphs. Such an approach doesn't acknowledge any
hierarchy or inheritance, plus the whole can be 'translated' without even
using object references.

I have no idea what the tools you have referred to are capable of doing -
but it seems to me, to take advantage of COBOL to Java - you will be faced
with a lot of chopping around of COBOL source code to dove tail into Java.
For most effective use of COBOL to Java, it seems to me you are faced with a
a systems re-write as a minimum, and then - do you fall into the trap of a
system re-design - but possibly you consider this latter to be an
'advantage' ?

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: COBO to Java translation

- **From:** chammond@lc.cc.il.us
- **Date:** 2001-06-27T15:31:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9hcu7k$g61$1@news.netmar.com>`
- **References:** `<3B394ED8.EEBA7F03@ican.net>`

```
In article <3B394ED8.EEBA7F03@ican.net>, Howard Wong  <hwong@ican.net>
writes:
>Hi All,
>
…[33 more quoted lines elided]…
>
You may want to check out the website at newsone.net they have html access to
the newsgroups.  

COBOL doesn't lend itself well to converting to OO from a non-OO platform. 
You may want to look for some tools that can look for perform statements or
make lists of the paragraphs (blocks of reusable code)  Often the GO TO
statements represent all of the key logic points and there seem to be no
blocks of reusable code.  But even a GO TO has to have a Label.  You are not
going to find many products that can take into account all of the redefines
and the occurs in a nice neet way.  Ive seen some Payroll files with occurs
inside of occurs that would drive a database conversion nuts.  Wait till you
find a file with 9 differrent record types, each with their own occurs for a 
600 character record!  Then there are the fields that hold different data at
different times in the processing stages.

You should just hire an experienced COBOL programmer as a consultant.  Often
these older programs are very old and they are not rewritten because the
orgranization did not want to spend the time or money to reengineer them. The
top-down approach works; it is just different.  

The use of Corresponding is a matter of taste and style.  If you use it that
means 2 things have to correspond.  That has always seemed to mean that much
more could go wrong when you have to change an existing program.  Many
programmers stay away from using corresponding.  

Varying is a fun one too.  It is often used to do things in a loop with a
counter, like a for loop.

You could just write CICS programs and use a screen scraper.

Have Fun.

>As for those tools out there that can automate the job, has anyone used
>any of them? If so, how effective were they? Was the generated Java code
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
>


 -----  Posted via NewsOne.Net: Free (anonymous) Usenet News via the Web  -----
  http://newsone.net/ -- Free reading and anonymous posting to 60,000+ groups
   NewsOne.Net prohibits users from posting spam.  If this or other posts
made through NewsOne.Net violate posting guidelines, email abuse@newsone.net
```

#### ↳ Re: COBO to Java translation

- **From:** thaneh@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2001-06-27T10:05:03-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bfdfc3e8.0106270905.a875118@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<3B394ED8.EEBA7F03@ican.net>`

```
Howard,

There's really no point to doing this.

One thing you can do is interface directly to the mainframe using
Sockets, or ECI (if CICS) and just use the application from the web
server.

If it must exist as a "bean" various COBOL vendors have tools that
will take the code and compile it - with the source remaining in COBOL
- into a Java Bean.

Alternately you can use the .NET Compiler from Fujitsu (free beta
download at adtools.com) to create a web service from the code.

The language it is written in is becoming largely irrelevant - what
matters is the connection to the web server (whatever it is) and the
security of the transaction.

Howard Wong <hwong@ican.net> wrote in message news:<3B394ED8.EEBA7F03@ican.net>...
> Hi All,
> 
…[50 more quoted lines elided]…
> hhjwong@netscape.net
```

#### ↳ Re: COBO to Java translation

- **From:** "Ira D. Baxter" <idbaxter@semdesigns.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-29T18:42:58-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3b3d17b7$1_1@feed1.realtime.net>`
- **References:** `<3B394ED8.EEBA7F03@ican.net>`

```
Others have remarked already on whether this is a good idea or not.
My initial reaction generally matches others.

However, Howard's organization may have good reasons to do this,
even if it is complicated or difficult.   One good reason is that
the business rules already encoded, while not priceless,
are enormously valuable, and automated conversion
might translate these reliably.   Hand-conversion likely won't.

I was under the impression that there was a COBOL-to-Java translator
out there, if you didn't mind the unreadability of the result.
No further comment.

If you want to do a custom translation, the DMS Software Reengineering
Toolkit
might be the way to go.  It offers generalized compiler technology
that can parse langauges, carry out arbitrary analyses and/or
transformations,
and then prettyprint the result.    DMS is already capable of
parsing/transforming/prettyprinting
both COBOL and Java, and can handle both at the same moment.

What is left is decide which part of the COBOL to extract, and
to write transforms that map between the languages.
This isn't easy (although having DMS take care of all that other
stuff means you can concentrate on just this).

See http://www.semdesigns.com/Products/DMS/DMSToolkit.html.
```

Hi, Liam,

Yes, we use Kedit with all of the current versions of Windows,
including WinMe.  We haven't tested it with XP yet, but I don't
anticipate any difficulties.

We're running version 1.5, service level 3 and have had no problems at
all.  I use it on Win2K Professional and Win98 about 10 hours daily,
and often run Kedit without closing it for weeks.  We haven't updated
it for years.

I could post the profiles and color settings that we use on our web
site, if anyone's interested.

Kevin
Norcom - COBOL Programming Tools
GUI ScreenIO - A Windows UI for COBOL
http://www.screenio.com

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Any good editor for Windows?

- **From:** "Editor" <Editor@aboutlegacycoding.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T16:45:10-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9hg4uv$22bk$1@allday.news.cais.net>`
- **References:** `<5k2kjtkgd0844vhp6hj84f2v1cflui3iqq@4ax.com> <3b3a55b8.190500154@news> <3B3A6CB3.D3616D6C@optonline.net>`

```
We use MultiEdit - www.multiedit.com
This editor is HIGHLY configurable and will edit HTML, Basic, C, etc.
including color coding, intelligent indents, etc. for each of these
languages.  It even has context sensitive help for some languages like HTML.

For years, to get MultiEdit configured for COBOL you needed the Macro's
created by The Progeni Corporation  www.progeni.com which are still
available for free (in case you own an older version of MultiEdit) on their
website or in the download library at www.aboutlegacycoding.com.  However
the newest version of MultiEdit, version 9, ships with the Progeni COBOL
Macros built in.

We run MultiEdit on all the Windows platforms up to and including 2000.
```

Do a search on comp.lang.cobol and this subject on groups.google.com.  There
was a discussion on this topic in this newsgroup a few weeks ago.

I use VIM (Vi IMproved) from www.vim.org.  It's an open source vi clone (a
Unix editor), with many improvements and has syntax highlighting for many
languages.  There is a Win32 version - both a GUI and console version.  I
use the console version.  I do not use syntax highlighting - I use it
because I know and like vi.

## Thread: Fujitsu 'News' — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Fujitsu 'News'

- **From:** "Jerry P" <jerryp@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-27T22:04:25+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<JLs_6.482941$ho6.31259873@news5.aus1.giganews.com>`

```
I received an email today which read, in part:

--- begin quote
To all,


In a cost cutting measure, Fujitsu Software has not renewed the
contracts for its independent consultants.  June 29 will be my last
day here.

It has been a pleasure working with you all.  I've enjoyed the little
puzzles and problems you presented.  I derive great satisfaction from
solving them.

In the future please address your support questions directly to
support@adtools.com or even better, sign on to the new support tool on
the www.adtools.com web site.

If you run into a problem Fujitsu Software cannot address, I am
available for contract work.  I can be contacted via email at (name
and address)

--- end quote


Gosh.

I thought, what with their new licensing scheme and all, Fujitsu would
be taking money to the bank in wheelbarrows. Could still be true and
this was not a cost-cutting measure, but some other, unsaid, reason.

The world waits.
```

### OT - NZ Culture, was "comp.std.cobol"

- **From:** stephenjspiro@hotmail.com (Stephen J Spiro)
- **Date:** 2001-06-27T20:31:05-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<4145699b.0106271931.53c7f9a8@posting.google.com>`

```
An American is travelling through the countryside of New Zealand
enjoying the beautiful scenery. Suddenly out of the corner of his eye
he sees a young man having his way with a sheep in a field. Disgusted
he pulls into a pub only to find a man with one leg masturbating at
the end of the bar. Horrified he says to the barman "What the fuck is
wrong with this country, first I see a guy fucking a sheep and now
there's an amputee jerking off in public." The barman calmly replies
"Fair go mate, you can't expect him to catch a sheep with one leg."
```

<a id="t-bb548e5a24"></a>
## Thread: From TRUNC(STD) to TRUNC(BIN) — 5 msgs

### From TRUNC(STD) to TRUNC(BIN)

- **From:** esimo@unibank.dk (Elo Simonsen)
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T05:49:17-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ff4e264c.0106280449.41f890f0@posting.google.com>`

```
At our site we're going to change the 'trunc' compiler-option from
trunc(std) to trunc(bin) for all batch-programs (we already use the
trunc(bin)-setup for cics-programs).
Do any of you have any experiences regarding this change (i.e.
behaviour of  'on size error' for add-instructions) ?
Or is this just a straightforeward change.

Our compiler: IBM Cobol for OS/390

Regards Elo Simonsen,  Nordea

PS: We know the differences between the trunc-options, so please don't
use any of your time explaining those to us.
```

#### ↳ Re: From TRUNC(STD) to TRUNC(BIN)

- **From:** Binyamin Dissen <postingid@dissensoftware.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T18:41:33+03:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<6ljmjt85dv0v84efrf31pu93n9qabijdt2@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<ff4e264c.0106280449.41f890f0@posting.google.com>`

```
On 28 Jun 2001 05:49:17 -0700 esimo@unibank.dk (Elo Simonsen) wrote:

:>At our site we're going to change the 'trunc' compiler-option from
:>trunc(std) to trunc(bin) for all batch-programs (we already use the
:>trunc(bin)-setup for cics-programs).
:>Do any of you have any experiences regarding this change (i.e.
:>behaviour of  'on size error' for add-instructions) ?
:>Or is this just a straightforeward change.

:>Our compiler: IBM Cobol for OS/390

As the ON SIZE ERROR clause applies to the intermediate field, i.e. it is
examined before the result field is stored, I would not expect any change in a
well behaved compiler.
```

#### ↳ Re: From TRUNC(STD) to TRUNC(BIN)

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T14:01:26-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9hfuua$vo1$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<ff4e264c.0106280449.41f890f0@posting.google.com>`

```
I assume that you know that IBM *strongly* recommends against using
TRUNC(BIN) as the default.  This is true for a NUMBER of reasons - but
certainly is true for ANY performance sensitive applications.

Have you looked at TRUNC(OPT) which gives you "similar" results for many
(most?) situations - but with SIGNIFICANTLY better performance?  With IBM
COBOL for OS/390 & VM V2R2, it is even possible to use TRUNC(OPT) as your
default and to get TRUNC(BIN) behavior for SPECIFIC fields that need it.
This is done by defining them as USAGE COMP-5  (see the "What's new"
sections in the current LRM and programming guides).

If (for some reason) you are REQUIRED to go to TRUNC(BIN) as the shop
default, then I would strongly suggest that you look at some of the examples
in the Programming Guide on when you will get different results from the
same statements.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: From TRUNC(STD) to TRUNC(BIN)

- **From:** Volker Bandke <vbandke@bsp-gmbh.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T23:01:41+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<h66njtg3v353a6i6r5rdefqfq38ac1f3vu@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<ff4e264c.0106280449.41f890f0@posting.google.com> <9hfuua$vo1$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Bill,

while the facts you wrote is perfectly correct, I don't think the
advice you gave is so <s>

AFAIK, TUNC(OPT) gives a more or less undefined result - the compiler
will do whatever is best performance-wise, which means it will
truncate sometimes and not on other times (at least that is howI
interpret the manual)

Unfortunately, a lot of IBM copy booke (for CICS, for DB2, for
whatever) have COMP-4 not COMP-5 as the useage clause in field
declarations.  I would feel very uncomfortable with a TRUNC(OPT)
setting under these circumstances

The same is true for assembler subroutines that are called with
parameters in  S9(4) COMP-4 (standardized copy books, e.g.)

Better safe than (fast and) sorry



On Thu, 28 Jun 2001 14:01:26 -0500, "William M. Klein"
<wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com> wrote:

>I assume that you know that IBM *strongly* recommends against using
>TRUNC(BIN) as the default.  This is true for a NUMBER of reasons - but
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>same statements.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: From TRUNC(STD) to TRUNC(BIN)

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T22:22:08-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9hgs91$861$1@slb1.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<ff4e264c.0106280449.41f890f0@posting.google.com> <9hfuua$vo1$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net> <h66njtg3v353a6i6r5rdefqfq38ac1f3vu@4ax.com>`

```
IBM has been pretty conflicting on whether they do or do NOT
recommend/require TRUNC(BIN) for CICS applications.  At one time (and I
think is some places still) they recommend doing so.  In other places, the
recommend against it.

Clearly if you are using IBM COBOL of OS/390 & VM V2R2 and do copy in DFH
copybooks (or EXEC SQL INCLUDEs) and *want* to guarantee TRUNC(BIN) behavior
for such field, then you might consider adding

   replacing ==COMP-4== by ==COMP-5==
                 ==COMP==    by  == COMP-5==
          .

The point of the COMP-5 "enhancement" in V2R2 is to ALLOW (encourage?) use
of TRUNC(OPT) or (STD) as the application default and limit fields that
actually require "true binary" to COMP-5.
```

---

<a id="t-aefab65d97"></a>
## Thread: Where's the COBOL world headed? — 5 this month / 10 total

### Where's the COBOL world headed?

- **From:** "David P. Bretz" <DBretz@mescoma.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T12:23:37-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B3B5A09.DA7774E3@mescoma.com>`

```
Maybe this isn't the place to post this sort of message, but I felt like
venting and thought maybe some of you might have been in the same
situation as I'm in now.

I am a mainframe applications developer.  I have been doing this sort of
work (COBOL, CICS, VSAM, DB2, etc., etc., etc.) for 17 years.  I love
turning ideas into working code.  I think it's great, and most of the
clients I've had think I'm great at it.

Recently, due to circumstances beyond my control, the niche that my
little consulting company I started with a friend of mine 7 years ago
had, has sort of dried up.  So, we're now looking for some work as
individuals again.  I thought that it would be nice to find something
local, since I've been on the road for 10 years, and sort of missed my
family.

Anyway.......I've been doing all the normal job search things......going
to the web, looking at all the job boards, sending my resume out via
e-mail, snail mail, whatever they want.  I've been talking to
recruiters, friends, relatives, enemys.....everyone I can think of
talking to.  And, I've made some fascinating discoveries.

First.........an e-mailed resume is really easy to ignore.   Of all the
e-mails I've sent, I have not yet gotten a response.   Not even a
negative response.

Second.....with some companies, it's almost impossible to actually talk
to a human being.  There is one company (I won't mention the name in
public) that I've called, e-mailed and called again, and I either get
answering machines or automated e-mail back.  I have yet to talk to a
real live person.  I find the same attitude in other places as well.
They're "annoyed" that they actually have to talk to someone.

Third.......if you've been programming for a long time, and you're
making a good salary doing it.......don't change jobs!!!     Corporate
america is not interested in experience.  All they want are people with
2-5 years of experience that they can pay $40-50,000.  I suppose I
shouldn't be surprised.  I've seen it in every company I worked for as a
consultant.  They would give an assignment to one of their employees
because they said it would cost too much for me to do it........and it
would take the employee 2 weeks to do what I could do in 4 hours
(application expertise does have it's benefits).   Now, where's the
fiscal intelligence in something like that?

Anyway.............I know lots of you are out there, and some of you
must have changed jobs in the recent past.........what have your
experiences been?

Thanks

Dave Bretz
```

#### ↳ Re: Where's the COBOL world headed?

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T17:33:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B3B6A45.480E87C5@home.com>`
- **References:** `<3B3B5A09.DA7774E3@mescoma.com>`

```


"David P. Bretz" wrote:

> Maybe this isn't the place to post this sort of message, but I felt like
> venting and thought maybe some of you might have been in the same
> situation as I'm in now.

David,

I can't offer a solution - but ironically in today's paper, primarily Canada
but it did include the current downturn in the US :-

"Tumultuous times have hit three of Canada's biggest job sectors: the tech
industry, the oilpatch and the airline industry. High-tech companies (me -
such as Nortel), are laying off huge numbers of employees, but at the same
time are mounting massive recruiting efforts for specialized workers. The
airline industry is turning away record numbers of job applicants for
entry-level positions, but forecasting a shortage of pilots and
mechanics.....".

Then goes on covering other industries saying there is a glut of entry-level
people, but they are missing the 'seniors' with the experience. Parallel
situation in the States. Now that all just doesn't jive with your currently
difficulties does it.

First thing I would suggest - those bastards you are e-mailing, they should
all be sent to a forced labour camp and be brainwashed to learn Etiquette
101 ! Then maybe the second subject should be Experience 101.

As you say you lost your niche. Give it some thought, and may require you to
get into something like Java to complement COBOL. But can you create a fresh
niche ?

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Where's the COBOL world headed?

- **From:** "David P. Bretz" <DBretz@mescoma.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T21:37:26-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B3BDBD6.3277703F@mescoma.com>`
- **References:** `<3B3B5A09.DA7774E3@mescoma.com> <3B3B6A45.480E87C5@home.com>`

```
Well, I do agree about the labor camp idea.   I think that would be great!
:-)

I appreciate your suggestions.  As it happens, I did take some steps toward
expanding my technical horizons after our last contract was not renewed.  My
partner and I talked to lots of different people and did some research on the
web, and the consensus was that it would be a good idea to do training in
Siebel's CRM systems, and get the Siebel certification.  Which is what we did.
Spent $20,000 to take the classes and live in Atlanta for 3 weeks.....studied
hard and passed the certification exam.

When I started posting my resume on the web, I got about a dozen nibbles for
people looking for Siebel developers.....but, when they found out I had no OTJ
experience with Siebel, they didn't want to talk to me.  Never mind 17 years of
programming experience and all that it teaches.  If I don't have 1 year of
actual Siebel training, it's like I'm fresh out of college again.  My personal
opinion is that it's a pretty myopic view of things.

I've also taken up VB on my own, and I do plan on XML and Java next.   Might as
well do something with my down time!

I've read some articles in US papers that the government here will lose 50% of
it's current senior IT staff in the next few years.  It will be interesting to
see what happens when that begins.

Dave

Can anyone out there help me, I am a 65 year old photographer running a
business on an old (12 years) version of Microfocus cobol for CCPM . I have
recently purchased Workbench V 3.1  and although my programs are compiling
OK, they are falling over at run time with errors I don't really understand.
It appears I have a lot more to do than with the older version, when I did
nothing. It is important to me as my livelehood ( and 5 others) is dependant
on me sorting it out. Has anyone the patience to help me through the next
few weeks. I am in the UK so it will be by e-mail I suppose.
```

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>

Mike Nicholson wrote:

> Can anyone out there help me, I am a 65 year old photographer running a
> business on an old (12 years) version of Microfocus cobol for CCPM . I have
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> few weeks. I am in the UK so it will be by e-mail I suppose.

Mike, you are a mere youngster <G> And even if you are from Blighty and say "Eh
oop lad", or "I say Old Chappie" we wont know from your writing. Spell out your
problems with some DETAILS and I'm sure you'll get help.

One quick tip - are you getting file errors when you attempt to run. Go to your
manuals and look at the detail of file-status codes and some of this is
associated with the way in which you are accessing files,  for example you can't
REWRITE if you have opened the file only as INPUT.  You should also find a table
referencing when you can/can't use which file VERBS with the various OPEN
statements associated with file type, i.e. Relative, Sequential ISAM(indexed).

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

but when I execute it I get the following result:


          Micro Focus COBOL/2 File Management Utility
          Version 1.1.17 Copyright (C) 1985,1988 Micro Focus Ltd.

          Conversion

[/i][/v]

my old database to a new one.

Thank you.

rebuild is a command line utility.  You have to tell
it what to do on the command line.

If you run with NO parms, it does this.
REBUILD FILENAME.DAT /I
  might get you the record size and key
locations, but not much else.

If this is an indexed file, MSACCESS is probably
not going to be able to read it.

<a id="t-a6b7fbc6ac"></a>
## Thread: ISO/IEC/NCITS/ANSI dinosaur. Was many threads — 17 msgs

_Topics: [`COBOL's future, legacy, and obsolescence`](topics.md#future)_

### ISO/IEC/NCITS/ANSI dinosaur. Was many threads

- **From:** stephenjspiro@hotmail.com (Stephen J Spiro)
- **Date:** 2001-06-28T14:08:47-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<4145699b.0106281308.7c097a02@posting.google.com>`

```
Okay, let's review the facts.

1) ISO/IEC/NCITS/ANSI are hidebound bureaucracies, totally
unresponsive to the people who use their products
2) They are a secretive elite, who, for their own secret reasons, want
to exclude the real users from having any significant, meaningful
input to their deliberations
3) They are locked into primitive procedures and have a luddite
aversion to modernizing anything.

We know these things are FACTS because respected, knowledgable
contributors to this newsgroup have said so, and they must know.

I just received the attached e-mail (the attachments are also
attached).  In light of the above FACTS, this confuses me terribly.

Stephen J Spiro

PS: If you want to participate, notify Russ Richards, chairman, at
richar1r@ncr.disa.mil  as soon as possible.
Ummmmm, this IS an internal NCITS experiment; I do not know just how
open it will be.  I suggest you indicate reasons for your interest if
you contact Mr Richards.
 


*------------------
> -----Original Message----- 
> From: Ryland, Jim 
…[14 more quoted lines elided]…
> 2001 - SEE ATTACHMENT !!!!!! 


*-------------------------------ATTACHMENT 1 --------------------

National Committee for Information Technology Standards
NCITS Secretariat, Information Technology Industry Council (ITI) 
1250 Eye St. NW, Suite 200, Washington, DC 20005 
Telephone 202-737-8888; Fax 202-638-4922; 
Email: ncits@itic.org 


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Doc: IT/01-0572 
Date: June 20,  2001 
Reply to: Russ Richards, Chair, Emerging Technologies Ad Hoc 
Phone: 703-898-8268 (wireless) 
Email: richar1r@ncr.disa.mil 

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To: Members, NCITS 

Subject: New Technologies Ad Hoc Committee, Mock Electronic Meeting,
Thursday, July 5, 2001, 1:00-3:00 P.M. EDT

Non-binding RSVP by June 30, 2001 



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Problem: 

1. Face-to-face meetings have some drawbacks: 
a. Travel costs. 
b. Productive time lost to travel. 

c. Host must commit significant time and expense to logistics.

2. NCITS decided to explore the potential of electronic meetings as an
occasional substitute for face-to-face meetings.
3. A electronic chat mock meeting is the appropriate way to do this.
Holding a real meeting as a test would put at risk timely conduct of
NCITS business if this initial electronic forum experiment is not
successful.
 

Exploring Solutions: 
1. The meeting will employ electronic chat technology already 
successfully used by a TC. 
2. The mock meeting will be short (2 hours), but in order for it to be
realistic it will follow an agenda and feature a single real subject
for discussion and deliberation.

3. The subject will be: "Electronic Meetings: Is This Meeting 
Working?" In other words, the meeting will discuss the meeting itself.

4. The mock meeting will be considered a realistic experiment if at
least 50 percent of the regular quarterly NCITS meeting attendees
participate. A good representative attendance is important because the
experiment is an NCITS Ad Hoc Committee assignment.  These membersï¿½
experience with the mock meeting will be used by NCITS as a basis for
possible revision to NCITS procedures, and their personal experiences
with this experiment will guide their own votes.
 

Assumptions: 
1. There will be people who believe that electronic meetings can be
used effectively;
2. There will be others who believe that electronic meetings cannot be
used effectively;

3. Therefore, active debate with opposing positions can be
anticipated, just like issues at a regular NCITS meeting.

4. All regular NCITS members and TC officers/members are invited to
participate.
 

Potential Questions to be discussed or explored at the meeting: 
1. How to adhere to an agenda in an environment where it is natural to
have several people "talking" (typing) at once? Is it still necessary
to maintain a speaking queue?
2. How would votes be conducted? 

a. Authentication by logon and password? 
b. Closing discussion and validating results with a decision record? 

c. Secret ballot by sending chair an email with confirmation of vote
acknowledged by chair online (not disclosing actual vote)?

3. Without experience in electronic meetings, how do we schedule an
appropriate amount of time for a topic?
a. Will topics typically take longer? 
b. Will topics typically take a shorter period of time? 
c. It would appear that we would need to conduct several meetings to
gain enough experience to answer these questions.
4. Does the chair need a special communication channel, or will
attendees naturally give the chair appropriate attention and
deference? Will the chair need enforcement mechanisms to control the
floor or maintain a speaking queue?
5. How to apply Roberts Rules of Order to such a meeting? 

6. An electronic chat meeting unavoidably allows capture of a
permanent record of all discussion. Is this a deterrent to open debate
and expression?


Logistics: 

1. Attendees will need only a connection to the Internet (28K is
adequate) and a standard Web browser. There is no need to make special
firewall provisions.
2. Two days before the meeting, as the host of this mock meeting,
Steve Haflich <smh@franz.com> will distribute instructions and the URL
that will be used.  The message will be sent to the NCITS distribution
list.
 

*-------------------------ATTACHMENT 2 -----------------------------
*-------------------------Item 2 is
relevant-----------------------------


IT/01-0427 
New Technologies Ad Hoc Committee 

1. Coordinate NCITS use of XML as a tool for document development and
publication.

        Representative interim objectives may include: 

Work with NCITS H2 as the demonstration project 
Retain a repository of lessons learned 
Coordinate the NCITS XML activity with external organizations (e.g.,
ANSI, ITTF)
Coordinate NCITS internal XML activities 
Make recommendations to NCITS on: 
- editing and rendering tools capable of generating the appropriate
format (e.g., PDF)
- a consistent means of persistently naming, indexing, and semantic
markup
- a common structure for XML in NCITS (e.g., schema/DTD) 
- a proposal for broad use of XML by NCITS (including the officially
approved version)
2. Prepare recommendations for NCITS use of electronic meetings. 
        Representative interim tasks may include: 

Review NCITS procedures 
Prepare model procedures for electronic meetings 
Identify appropriate technologies 
Conduct a pilot meeting and report to NCITS on the results
```

#### ↳ Re: ISO/IEC/NCITS/ANSI dinosaur. Was many threads

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2001-06-29T12:46:09+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3b3bcfc8_6@Usenet.com>`
- **References:** `<4145699b.0106281308.7c097a02@posting.google.com>`

```
"Stephen J Spiro" <stephenjspiro@hotmail.com> wrote in message
news:4145699b.0106281308.7c097a02@posting.google.com...
> Okay, let's review the facts.
>
> 1) ISO/IEC/NCITS/ANSI are hidebound bureaucracies, totally
> unresponsive to the people who use their products
>
Yep, that's about the size of it. Glad you've seen the light, Stephen <G>.

I never heard of NCITS until today and have NEVER included them in my
comments.

My concern is ONLY with the bodies who are the Guardians of COBOL.

Looks like NCITS are either more responsive or they have simply realised
that it is the 21st Century and the technology to put them in touch with
their users is available.

Fully endorse their approach, but I'd be interested to know how much
empowerment they will give to the contributors.

> 2) They are a secretive elite, who, for their own secret reasons, want
> to exclude the real users from having any significant, meaningful
> input to their deliberations

I don't think there is any secret regarding their reasons...control of COBOL
is a nice little earner. Giving control back to the users would diminish
(but not completely dry up) this revenue source.

As for an elite, well, I see it as being a lot like the days when
Gentlemen's sons could buy a commission in the British Army.  You bought
your commission and became an "Officer and a Gentleman".  Even such a
conservative organisation as the British Military realised over 100 years
ago that this is NOT the best way to get the leaders that you need....

Having said that, there was nothing especially wrong with many of the people
who bought their commissions and they died gladly for England, victims of
appalling mis-management, in the Napoleonic Wars, in the Empire, and the
Crimea.

We saw the final twitches of this system (it was officially phased out, but
the British "Class" structure replaced it) with the sacrifices made on the
Somme, Ypres, Passchendale, and, of course, Gallipoli, where a lot of young
Australians and New Zealanders became the victims of it.

The fact is that if you want something done properly, whether it is the
development of a Standard, or a campaign for Military Objectives, use the
people who are best qualified ON MERIT to do it.

An empowered PUBLIC forum can include Acadaemia, programmers who are using
COBOL every day in the workplace, vendors who rely on it for their
livelihood, Project Managers who know how to get things in on time and on
budget, a complete cross-section of interested parties who will all have
different things to contribute.  A closed door committee, which people buy
their way onto, by comparison DOES seem elitist.

> 3) They are locked into primitive procedures and have a luddite
> aversion to modernizing anything.
>

Their track record would certainly suggest so...


> We know these things are FACTS because respected, knowledgable
> contributors to this newsgroup have said so, and they must know.
>

Hahaha! No idea who you are referring to here... <G>

BTW, I have never claimed that anything I post here is anything other than
my OPINION. As this is an unmoderated NewsGroup I can freely express it, and
I do so (but then, I'm sure you noticed...<G>).

I leave it to the readership to decide what is FACT, what is rhetoric, what
is probably true but unprovable, and what is just downright mischief (but
fun...<G>)

There is no denying the FACT that it has taken more than 10 years to produce
a Standard document, and we will see no benefit from it before 2004. Forgive
me if that pisses me off....it does.

> I just received the attached e-mail (the attachments are also
> attached).  In light of the above FACTS, this confuses me terribly.
>

There, there, Stephen, rest your poor head...<G>

ONE Standards body is starting to "get with the program".

(With your background in J4, I can understand how you would find this
"confusing" <G>)

I have some hesitation about using on-line chat, but I really hope it works
for them.

The point about a News Group Forum is that there is a complete record of
what each person said, there is time to consider the posts of others, and
you don't have the problem of many people talking at once, which is fairly
normal for a "chat" room. Nevertheless, I am gratified to see the NCITS
attitude...

Now what about the ones who control COBOL?

Pete


Posted Via Usenet.com Premium Usenet Newsgroup Services
----------------------------------------------------------
    ** SPEED ** RETENTION ** COMPLETION ** ANONYMITY **
----------------------------------------------------------        
                http://www.usenet.com
```

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz>

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3b3ca604_4@Usenet.com>`
- **References:** `<4145699b.0106281308.7c097a02@posting.google.com> <3b3bcfc8_6@Usenet.com> <4145699b.0106282319.340b0ff3@posting.google.com>`

```

"Stephen J Spiro" <stephenjspiro@hotmail.com> wrote in message
news:4145699b.0106282319.340b0ff3@posting.google.com...
> "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz> wrote
> > I never heard of NCITS until today and have NEVER included them in my
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>
OK, I didn't know that, but I have also read Bill's post clarifying the
whole structure and I can see how immovable and difficult it is.

The chances of anything changing are zilch. There are too many people to
pass bucks around...

>
> Ummmm, this means the results of the experiment will apply to COBOL.
> Now do you see why I am confused? If they do this, then my trust and
> faith in the Common Wisdom of comp.lang.cobol will be shattered.

I'm glad the result of the experiment will apply to COBOL although, as I
said before, I don't think a chat room is the answer. At least somebody is
TRYING to get into the 21st Century...

This is a start, but it has a long way to go before it becomes an empowered
forum.

If the establishment of a trial chat room for ONE session, means you feel
that what I have been saying here for the past few months is rendered
invalid, then you certainly ARE confused...<G>

As for your faith in the Common Wisdom of CLC being shattered because
somebody sees there is Common Sense in trying to become more communicative,
then that's a non sequitur...if someone agrees with what we've been saying,
then that should reinforce your faith in the Wisdom of it.

Irony and sarcasm aside, Stephen, the fact remains that NOBODY is moving to
open COBOL up in the way that has been suggested here and described at
length. It requires an empowered Forum in which EVERYONE can participate,
with the certain knowledge that what they say and do can make a difference.

Until the Standards Organisations move to do this, I shall remain sceptical
that they really have any interests other than their own at heart, and, that
being the case, they should be made to show cause WHY they should continue
to be entrusted with COBOL. Failure to respond should be seen as an
admission of having no case and, therefore, no credibility.

Once we can accept they are not credible, we should move to set up our own
COBOL standard, using the methods already discussed, and simply ignore what
they are doing.

I have given this problem a great deal of thought over the past few weeks,
and I believe I could produce a viable standards document for COBOL,
including OO, within 18 months, given a public forum with access to all
interested parties, and using what is already available. I say this because
I believe the waste of resources we have seen over the past 10 years is
mainly due to mis-management and outmoded procedures. There would be no
reason why people who are currently involved in the various committees
couldn't contribute if they wanted to, but the Management of it would be
different than currently:

1. NO closed door committees.
2. NO boondoggles from Corporate Sponsorship unless something really good
has been achieved.
3. ANSWERABILITY to ALL.
4. NO Membership or eligibility requirements. Equal rights for all.
5. A Timeboxed and iterated, interactive, approach with the document being
publicly visible as it proceeds.
6. Rigid adherence to SIMPLE ENGLISH, with examples given to replace
convoluted legalese.
7. Any revenue generated by the said document would be distributed equitably
to the people who worked on producing it.

Given all the above, why would such a Standard have any less credibility
than the non-existent one we have waited 10 years for?

We could call it the "COBOL User Base Standard (CUB Standard)".

If I was a Corporate Sponsor I know where I'd put my money...<G>

Pete.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: ISO/IEC/NCITS/ANSI dinosaur. Was many threads

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-29T20:51:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B3CEA2F.25C5B7F7@home.com>`
- **References:** `<4145699b.0106281308.7c097a02@posting.google.com> <3b3bcfc8_6@Usenet.com> <4145699b.0106282319.340b0ff3@posting.google.com> <3b3ca604_4@Usenet.com> <qd5%6.15921$C81.1197855@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`

```


Russell Styles wrote:

> "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz> wrote in
> message news:3b3ca604_4@Usenet.com...
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
>

To be fair NOBODY is hijacking the process, i.e. if you are referring to
J4, and WITHOUT DOUBT they are TRULY dedicated. It just so happens that
historically, that's the way it has turned out. Go back to Day One, as
Pete says it was "IBM and BRUNCH" - the machine vendors. Well that was
OK, there weren't any damned users who could comment on a non-existent
language. Now because of our mutual ability to communicate, (as opposed
to 40 years ago), we are questioning the process NOT the people.

Pete's suggestion for a COBOL Standards Forum is not an attempt to have
a coup d'etat; rather to expand on the use of expertise, at varying
levels, to get a larger input - and the more involved in commenting, on
very specific topics, the easier it is to consider COBOL 2004, after
2002 has happened. As the situation stands at the moment we can possibly
look at best for COBOL 2010 - God knows what will have happened in
computing by that date !

Nor is there any suggestion of extremism. Going for simple Anglo-Saxon
expressions, (where possible), to describe the meaning in the FCD can
only bring clarity to the document. You've made note of a USA weakness;
as an observer I see a  tendency to pick an obtuse word when
'inappropriate' (Think of the Pentagon guys and their mish-mash of the
language when informing us about the Gulf War). Of course you could take
clarity to extremes - there are appropriate Anglo-Saxon words for -
copulate, defecate, flatulate, fornicate - but we use the 'Latins'  'cos
they sound 'nicer'.

More on this topic when I respond to Bill's comments about the
difficulties of trying to get over the joint ANSI/ISO problem.

Jimmy
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: ISO/IEC/NCITS/ANSI dinosaur. Was many threads

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@home.com>
- **Date:** 2001-06-30T05:05:26+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3B3D5DDD.A1367CCB@home.com>`
- **References:** `<4145699b.0106281308.7c097a02@posting.google.com> <3b3bcfc8_6@Usenet.com> <4145699b.0106282319.340b0ff3@posting.google.com> <3b3ca604_4@Usenet.com> <qd5%6.15921$C81.1197855@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net> <3B3CEA2F.25C5B7F7@home.com> <L7b%6.1630$Hl1.146981@bgtnsc05-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`

```


Russell Styles wrote:

> > >     We need to have some way of preventing a dedicated
> > > minority from hijacking the process.  This is one of the
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
> speeded up.  But don't rush to judgment either.

Well shows you how easy it is to misread even the written word. Pete
twigged your drift immediately, but I missed it completely !

ASSUME - a COBOL Standards Forum got going, and initially it would start
probably very S-L-O-W-L-Y, perhaps an initial 30, then some time on 100,
150, 250, say all the way up to 500 plus, after a year or two years -
who knows just speculative. And I'm not talking about the CLC people
getting on board, rather the larger community of 3 million who do NOT
participate in this NewsGroup. Let's settle for the 500 figure, but
could be 1,000 or ....... ?  (Anybody any idea of how MANY people
submitted initial proposals that created COBOL 2002 or how many
commented on the FCD ? Probably we are going all the way back to '86 to
answer the first question.- but a round number wouldn't hurt)

I can't give you a true measure, but let's take R/W which we already are
familiar with. It comes up as a thread. Arbitrarily, 30 contributing to
that might be OK, but perhaps I'd like to see 100, even better 200 or
300. Now we are getting somewhere. (Participation depends upon the size
of the topic being discussed in the particular thread). If we find
OVERALL, that threads only attract, say 5 to 10 people - forget it - the
whole process was a wash-out.

Your contention is surely - it is better to leave it solely in the hands
of the 13 UNELECTED and knowledgeable people who comprise J4, because
I'm comfortable with what we have, rather  than 500 UNELECTED and
knowledgeable people - even though Pete's suggestion is the latter
should also be controlled by a voting process.

Note - my view is that J4 is NOT excluded. Certainly until an e-mailing
forum is 'proven' or drops dead, I still see J4 as the final authority.
Even if the e-mail approach is TOTALLY successful - we still need a
final authority. We may criticize  what our current government is doing,
but we prefer that they stick around.

Be assured it aint going nowhere FAST. Even if Pete is full of vinegar
and piss, I for one would definitely throw out an anchor to slow the
process down to a sensible pace.

As I misread you initially, do you want to clarify where you say, "But
don't rush to judgment either". To which 'judgment' are you referring ?

Jimmy
```

"Russell Styles" <rwstyles@worldnet.att.net> wrote in message
news:qd5%6.15921$C81.1197855@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net...
>
>
>     We need to have some way of preventing a dedicated
> minority from hijacking the process.

This is total paranoia.

Why would anyone want to "hijack the process" of producing a COBOL Standard?
(what are they gonna do?... "If you don't start using EVALUATE, the compiler
will die...")

What you are really saying is: "Well, we need to reserve the right to
override a democratic process if the result does not favour our
predisposition..."

In other words, NO democratic process at all, just an unelected elite going
through the motions of persuading the hoi polloi that it really IS
democratic (as long as you don't want anything we disagree with...<G>)

> This is one of the USA's
> biggest weakness - groups that ACTUALLY VOTE have much
> more political power than their numbers should rate.  We don't
> want
> to go from one extreme to another.

ROFL!

Please read this again... "...groups that ACTUALLY VOTE have much more
political power than their numbers should rate..."

More political power than whom?

The groups that DON'T VOTE? (That is the whole idea of Democracy...) It's
another reason why we must eliminate groups that CAN'T VOTE (which is the
current situation with regard to the User Base...), by empowering ALL
interested parties.

The power of a given group is determined by the number of votes it gets. If
you feel so strongly, make sure you vote and persuade others to too. If you
can't do that, you deserve to lose. That's what Democracy is all about,
although I never thought I'd have to spell it out for someone who lives In
America...<G>

The problem you are referring to is that many people have lost sight of the
Democratic process. They will NOT accept losing in the polls. Instead they
seek to impose their will on the majority by Terrorism, bomb threats, and
general anarchy. And there is really no need for it. The Democratic process
provides for losers (on this occasion) to get out there and persuade the
majority by reasoned argument, to their point of view. At the next election,
they get another chance. And so on.
(If, after many attempts you are unsuccessful in your persuasion, then
either change your mind about it, or simply accept that you win some, you
lose some. Either way, deal with it and move on...)

We are not talking Proportional Representation here (it is a reasonable
compromise when we are talking about the rights of minorities with regard to
living standards and the Law).

We are talking about a Computer Programming Language Standard which SHOULD
be whatever the majority of its Users decide they want. (Whether it
conflicts with MY or YOUR personal opinion or not.)

>
>     I am of course speaking about "religious" opinions, such
> as go to's, and many others.
>

The ONLY religious opinion I would ascribe to is the right of the COBOL User
Base to get the Language they want.

Are you seriously saying that if the majority of Users wanted GO TO, or
wanted it banned (your example), you would somehow "reserve the right" to
prevent them from having it. Why? Because you know better? "Well, it's for
their own good, right?".
(BTW, I could make a powerful case for BOTH points of view in this regard,
so the outcome would probably be "Best leave it as it stands..." The point
being that we would ONLY vote on things where there was a definitive split
in opinion and the compromise ("have both") was untenable.)

Not me. I say whatever we vote for we get. If it turns out to be a disaster,
fine, we'll fix it at the next vote...

(Why is it I always get a tingling of alarm in the back of my head when
people start telling me what they will do "for my own good"?) <G>

Pete.
